mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trajectory in real personal consumption expenditures and durable goods orders reduces equity risk premiums by confirming stable aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of rising demand do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 9.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates sufficiently to trigger wage growth expectations and consumer spending acceleration, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0 percent, or predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and new residential housing construction signals synchronized supply-side capacity growth and demand-side confidence, compressing discount rates and lifting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both INDPRO and HOUST rise concurrently, or interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising short-term policy rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month, or the slope coefficient of FEDFUNDS on forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly reflects corporate hiring demand and economic momentum, providing a leading signal for positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months consistently yield negative or flat 5-day SPX returns, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC of PAYEMS growth on forward returns never exceeds 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts exceed 500 thousand units annually, the housing sector crosses a critical confidence threshold that boosts construction-related earnings and consumer wealth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where HOUST surpasses 500, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 0.5% over a 2-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment and stagnant industrial output indicates a demand-supply mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive when UNRATE rises and INDPRO declines simultaneously, or the interaction coefficient in a factor model is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In post-recession recovery phases, accelerating industrial production reflects pent-up demand absorption and capacity utilization improvements, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO accelerates during a defined recovery window, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
